Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Heartland" or "Zara Okoye" or "damage claim" or "crating" to find the Heartland pushback email (email_heartland_kwan_okoye_claim_001) from lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com, subject "Zara Okoye Damage Claim - Crating Specification Dispute". The email disputes the damage claim, arguing the work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE specifies "standard foam crating" not "anti-vibration foam crating", and that the $1,200 surcharge on INV-2026-0411 was for standard crating only.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "prototype" or "damaged" or "Mosaic" to find the broader damage complaint chain: mosaic_damage_complaint_001 from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (URGENT: Damaged Robotic Arm Prototype), mosaic_damage_ack_001 (Emeka's acknowledgment), and mosaic_incident_report_final_001 (incident report referencing HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, anti-vibration foam crating expectation, delivery signed by intern Kyle Britten at 9:15 AM, Zara's inspection at ~1:00 PM revealing damage, $90K damage to Prototype Unit #1, $40K gap between insurance coverage and full value).
OE 3: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Zara Okoye" or "damage claim" or "Mosaic" or "Heartland" to find internal discussions in #operations (C006), including Chloe Vance's message directing coordinators to reconcile Airtable records and specifically flagging Zara Okoye showing "Completed" while the damage claim is unresolved, and Emeka Diallo's message reporting Tamara Byrd's escalation threat: Mosaic will escalate to legal if no incident report and remediation plan by end of week (May 1). Emeka's message has a thread reply from Chloe Vance (thread_ts 1777159800.001000) revealing that Chloe had an off-record call with Lisa Kwan at Heartland: Heartland would accept a 60/40 liability split (them 60, us 40) if MoveOps can produce the original client specification sheet from Mosaic showing anti-vibration crating was explicitly requested. Chloe instructs Blessing not to accept Heartland's framing that the work order is the only relevant document and to push for the 60/40 split.
OE 4: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Zara Okoye" to find record recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta with Status = "Completed", Company = "Mosaic Robotics", Origin = "Savannah, GA", Destination = "Atlanta, GA", Assigned Coordinator = "Blessing Okafor". The Special Requirements field contains details about $180K prototypes, anti-vibration crating requirement, $1,200 surcharge, and an appended DAMAGE CLAIM block referencing the crating verification failure.
OE 5: Search CRM using crm_search_companies with name "Mosaic Robotics" to find the company record (company_mosaic), then search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with company_id "company_mosaic" to find deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026 (dealname contains "Mosaic Robotics" and "Zara Okoye Relocation" and "Savannah" and "Atlanta", dealstage "inprogress") and deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk ("Mosaic Robotics -- Zara Okoye Relocation (Savannah to Atlanta) [RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM]", dealstage "inprogress").
OE 6: Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria for DocNumber "INV-2026-0411" or customer "Mosaic" to find invoice inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc (DocNumber INV-2026-0411, TotalAmt $9,010) with line items including the $1,200 surcharge for Zara Okoye. The QB invoice line item description says "Custom Prototype Crating + Climate Monitoring" and specifies "anti-vibration foam crating" -- this contradicts Heartland's email claim that their work order only specifies "standard foam crating." The agent should notice this discrepancy: MoveOps's own invoice describes the service as anti-vibration crating, while Heartland claims they only contracted for standard crating. Agent may also discover credit memo cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001 (CM-2026-0415, -$90,000) for the damaged prototype.
OE 7: Reply to the Heartland email using reply_to_email with email_id "email_heartland_kwan_okoye_claim_001", sender "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", CC ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"], with content addressing the crating specification dispute, referencing the work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, the $1,200 surcharge, the anti-vibration crating expectation from the Special Requirements, and MoveOps's position on liability. The reply should reference the original client specification sheet from Mosaic and/or the proposed 60/40 liability split per Chloe's internal guidance (from the Slack thread reply). Alternative: send_email to lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com with CC chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 8: Update the Zara Okoye Airtable record using airtable_update_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id "tblRelocations01", records containing id "recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta" with Status changed from "Completed" to a non-completed status (e.g., "In Progress", "On Hold", or similar) to reflect the open damage claim.
OE 9: Create a CRM engagement note on the Mosaic Robotics account using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_mosaic"], and body documenting Tamara Byrd's escalation threat, specifically that Mosaic will escalate to legal if no incident report and remediation plan by May 1, referencing the crating specification dispute with Heartland and the $90K damage to Prototype Unit #1.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Carmen" or "UrbanNest" or "Marcus Webb" or "apartment" or "lease" to find both emails from carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com: (1) email_urbannest_reyes_webb_apartment_001, subject "Marcus Webb Chicago Apartment - Lease Start Date Conflict", presenting two options: (A) keep May 1 at $3,200/month with 4-day gap, or (B) push to May 5 but lose the unit; and (2) email_urbannest_reyes_webb_apartment_002, a follow-up sent April 26 presenting a third option: (C) May 3 start at $2,800/month (reduced from $3,200), 2-day gap instead of 4, $4,800 annual savings, but this option expires end of day Monday April 27. The agent must find both emails to have the complete picture.
OE 11: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Swift" or "rate" or "cold-chain" or "Marcus Webb" to find email_swift_pallone_rate_increase_001 from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com. Key facts: 18% rate increase effective May 1, 2026; original quote for 3 cryogenic lab items = $1,200, valid if shipment picked up by April 30; if delayed past April 30, new rate = $1,416 (increase of $216). The email also flags the Keiko Tanaka weight discrepancy (400 lbs certificate vs 420 lbs manifest).
OE 12: Email Carmen Reyes back using reply_to_email with email_id "email_urbannest_reyes_webb_apartment_002" (the latest email), sender "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", with content that addresses the Option C offer (May 3 at $2,800/month). The agent should either accept Option C for the cost savings, or explicitly choose Option A (May 1 at $3,200) while acknowledging Option C exists. The key test is whether the agent found Carmen's follow-up email and factored it into the decision. Alternative: reply_to_email with email_id "email_urbannest_reyes_webb_apartment_001", or send_email to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com.
OE 13: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with a query related to "Tanaka" or "DOT" or "documentation" to find issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (title contains "DOT Docs", "Keiko Tanaka", "Mosaic", "Class 3B Laser"), state = in progress, assignee = Chloe Vance (moveops_chloe_vance). Description covers Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter, Columbus OH to Seattle WA, DOT 49 CFR compliance, Swift Relocations as carrier.
OE 14: List comments on the Keiko Tanaka Linear issue using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic" to find comment_javier_tanaka_bol_gap_001 by Javier Morales identifying a second documentation gap: in addition to the missing placarding certificate, the move file also lacks a signed Bill of Lading with hazmat classification markings per DOT 49 CFR 172.200. The BOL in file is unsigned and missing Class 3B laser hazmat markings.
OE 15: Cross-reference Greg Pallone's email (from OE 11) using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_swift_pallone_rate_increase_001" to confirm the weight discrepancy details: placarding certificate lists Epilog Fusion Pro 48 at 400 lbs, but shipping manifest from Columbus pickup on April 15 recorded 420 lbs (20 lb difference). Certificate must match manifest before PHMSA submission.
OE 16: Create a comment on the existing Linear ticket using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic" and body summarizing the full scope of outstanding documentation: (1) missing signed placarding certificate, (2) weight discrepancy, certificate says 400 lbs vs manifest 420 lbs, needs correction before PHMSA submission, (3) missing signed Bill of Lading with hazmat classification markings per DOT 49 CFR 172.200.
OE 17: Reply to Greg Pallone using reply_to_email with email_id "email_swift_pallone_rate_increase_001", sender "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", confirming the weight discrepancy needs correction (the 420 lbs manifest weight should be used), requesting the amended placarding certificate, and asking him to also send the signed Bill of Lading with hazmat classification markings. Alternative: send_email to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com.
OE 18: Post a comprehensive update in the #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C006" and payload covering all three situations: (1) Zara Okoye: Heartland crating dispute status, liability position, Mosaic escalation threat, Airtable record corrected, CRM engagement logged; (2) Marcus Webb: apartment lease decision, Swift rate increase risk ($216 if shipment slips past April 30), lab dock delay to May 5; (3) Keiko Tanaka: DOT documentation gaps (placarding certificate, weight discrepancy 400 vs 420 lbs, missing signed BOL), Linear ticket updated, Greg Pallone contacted for corrected documents.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
